source_jobs.title: Pediatrician - CA License
source_jobs.description:
Clarity Pediatrics http://www.claritypediatrics.com is reimagining pediatric chronic care, starting with ADHD. Nearly 10% of people in the US are diagnosed with ADHD, and for parents, it can feel impossible to find the right care. We are building a virtual pediatric ADHD clinic that delivers trusted care to all families. Our new clinical model is tech-enabled, backed by the best science, and has the potential to deliver exceptional health outcomes to transform a child’s trajectory. In the future, our pediatric care platform will expand rapidly to other common chronic conditions like asthma, allergies, and obesity.



Our co-founders and leadership team have deep commitment to clinical excellence and domain expertise in healthcare. Our CEO, Christina LaMontagne https://www.linkedin.com/in/christina-lamontagne-a289152/, has led two startups and earned her MBA at Harvard Business School. Our Chief Medical Officer, Alesandro Larrazabal, MD https://www.linkedin.com/in/alesandro-larrazabal-m-d-7b4a9623/, trained at UCSF and Stanford. Until recently, he was a Medical Director building specialty care services at Kaiser Permanente. Earlier, he worked at IDEO.


What makes this role unique:



Opportunity to join a multi-disciplinary clinical team dedicated to providing integrated care to patients and families. We aim to give our providers  the tools they need to provide excellent care including collaboration with peers, guidance from ADHD experts and researchers, and opportunities to pursue ongoing professional development.



Contribute to the development of a better pediatric  healthcare system for patients and providers. Our team is dedicated to setting clinicians up for success, and you’ll be encouraged to help us make improvements to our care models, workflows, and clinical tools in order to make your work easier and more enjoyable.



Focus on your strengths. We appreciate how different clinical backgrounds help us learn from each other and take better care of patients. We encourage our clinicians to develop special areas of interest and expertise to become leaders within the team. We provide ongoing training opportunities to gain new skills as well as stipends for additional learning and development.



Responsibilities: 

 - Provide comprehensive clinical services for children and teens with ADHD and their families, with a focus on diagnostic and medication management.

 - Collaborate with a multidisciplinary team that includes MD’s, nurse practitioners, psychologists and other clinicians to provide excellent care.

 - Complete administrative work and clinical documentation in a timely and accurate manner.

 - Contribute to implementation, evaluation and program development iteration to ensure optimal delivery of care.

 - This is a clinical position, but there may be opportunities to expand and grow into program development, and other leadership activities, depending on employee interest and company needs.



Qualifications:

 - Licensed to practice medicine in California

 - Prescribing treatment plans including administering and managing ADHD stimulant medication

 - Board certified in Pediatrics

 - At least 5 years of patient care experience

 - Experience working with families from different cultural backgrounds and willingness to receive ongoing training and support in advancing your skills in delivering culturally responsive treatment

 - Interest or experience in Lifestyle Medicine. We emphasize evidence-based interventions that support children and families such as sleep hygiene, nutritional coaching and physical activity

 - Confidence in using an electronic medical record and telemedicine tools to deliver virtual care and support to families

 - Excitement about joining a startup and interest in learning new technologies and tools



Nice to have:

 - Licensed in additional states or open to obtaining additional licenses through support from Clarity Pediatrics Medical Group

 - Spanish-speaking



Job Type: Full-tim

source_jobs.title: Senior Full-Stack Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CLARITY PEDIATRICS

Clarity Pediatrics is reimagining pediatric chronic care, starting with ADHD. Nearly 10% of children in the US are diagnosed with ADHD, and for parents, finding the right care can feel impossible. We're building a virtual pediatric ADHD clinic that delivers trusted, science-backed care to all families — and it's working. We have strong product-market fit, a growing patient base, and a clear path to expanding into other common chronic conditions like asthma, allergies, and obesity.

Our leadership team has deep healthcare expertise. Our CEO has led two startups to >$100M in ARR and profitability. Our Chief Medical Officer trained at UCSF and Stanford and previously built specialty care services at Kaiser Permanente. Clarity is female-led — our CEO, Head of Engineering, and product leadership are all women building a company where diverse perspectives drive better care and better technology.

We've raised $14.5M in Series A capital from top VCs who have backed category-defining health-tech companies like One Medical, Hims, Spring Health, Cityblock, Honor, and Tia.




WHY THIS ROLE, WHY NOW

This is a rare combination: a mission-driven company with proven traction, a small team where your work is immediately visible, and a genuine mandate to push AI forward — using it within the product to solve our biggest problems and improve the care we deliver.

You'll have outsized impact. You'll be the sixth engineer on our team — on a group this small at a Series A company, every person moves the needle. You'll own meaningful product areas end-to-end, ship directly to patients and clinicians, and see the results of your work in real care outcomes.

You'll do real 0→1 work. Expanding into new conditions and new states means building products from scratch, not just iterating on what's there. And you'll do it on a stable, well-maintained codebase — so your energy goes to creating, not firefighting.

You'll shape how we build. You won't just contribute — you'll set direction. You'll define engineering culture, development practices, architecture, and quality standards as we grow. Your technical judgment will directly shape the team we become.

You'll drive AI strategy, not just use AI tools. AI is central to our roadmap — both in how we build and what we ship. This role has real latitude to experiment, advocate, and influence how AI shows up across our product and our software development lifecycle.




OUR STACK

 - Web: Next.js (React), TypeScript, Modern CSS

 - Backend: Node.js / Next API Routes

 - Data: PostgreSQL, Redis

 - Testing: Vitest, React Testing Library, Playwright, Github Actions

 - Infrastructure: Aptible (Experience with AWS or GCP is a plus)




WHAT YOU’LL DO

Own Features End-to-End: Take a loosely defined problem — a gap in the patient experience, a clinician workflow that's too manual, an internal tool that doesn't exist yet — and drive it from requirements through architecture, development, and delivery. You'll collaborate directly with product and clinical stakeholders to ship high-impact work.

Drive AI Forward: Identify where AI can improve care delivery and accelerate development. Experiment boldly, share what you learn, and level up the team alongside you — all within HIPAA-compliant tooling, so you can push hard without trading away patient privacy.

Build Across the Stack: Develop features across our patient-facing apps, pediatrician dashboards, and internal clinical tools. You'll go where the problem takes you and make sound architectural decisions along the way.

Set Technical Direction: Own and improve our engineering processes — CI/CD, code quality, testing strategy, API and database design. Your architectural judgment and technical decisions will define the standards the team builds on.

Move Fast, Stay Secure: Ship high-velocity code while maintaining the rigorous security and privacy standards required for HIPAA-compliant healthcare.

Mentor and Raise the Bar: Elevate the team

source_jobs.title: Behavioral Health Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Clarity Pediatrics

Clarity Pediatrics https://url.emailprotection.link/?b_geuVr7wJhtChd17oSZXHmPq-1ItFoIiId_ntxOBF8iFVdhm7lCHJ686zuiz-8H2zZL85j8dCst8H32rc-M7h_dkgXHr_6Kji8wYv1tr4K0vYFs1KlzU9yOVXvJJtd_S is redefining pediatric chronic care in a fragmented $100B industry.
Clarity is fast emerging as one of the most innovative leaders in pediatric health. One in three American children has a common chronic condition like ADHD, anxiety, allergies, asthma, and obesity – yet families struggle to get timely, high-quality care. We scale virtual access to multi-specialty care, putting all the care a family needs in one place. We are turning a chaotic, fragmented and expensive care journey into a clear path forward.

Now is a great time to join - we just closed a substantial Series A, which means we have runway, ambition and a growing team. Since inception, we’ve raised $25M from leading VCs who invested early in category winners like One Medical, Hims, Spring Health, Cityblock, Alto Pharmacy, Strava, and Tia.

Our Culture


WE’RE BUILDING A TEAM OF CLINICIANS WHO ARE COLLABORATIVE, CURIOUS, AND COMMITTED TO DELIVERING EXCEPTIONAL CARE. HERE’S WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT:

 - Co-founded by a pediatric provider, with exceptional clinical leaders including licensed mental health providers in key roles shaping every major decision

 - Clinical model built to partner closely with referring providers

 - Multidisciplinary collaboration and robust case consultation opportunities

 - A diverse, inclusive, and mission-driven community where your voice matters

 - Access to expert clinical guidance from ADHD and anxiety specialists and researchers

 - Ongoing opportunities for professional growth and training

About the role:

In this high-impact role, you’ll report to our Executive Clinical Leadership team and lead a growing team of behavioral health providers. You’ll work closely with our operations partners to ensure care runs smoothly, while driving clinical quality, enhancing the patient experience, and helping to shape a mission-driven team and care model. This position is fully remote, with optional in-person work days in the Bay Area, CA.

If you're passionate about leadership, team culture, and delivering exceptional behavioral health care, we’d love to connect.


ABOUT YOU:

 - Proven track record solving operational challenges in behavioral health or similar clinical settings

 - Balances empathy for staff with structure, accountability, and clear boundaries

 - Comfortable with ambiguity; sees opportunity in fast-changing environments

 - Natural leader who empowers teams and raises the bar

 - Licensed Clinical Psychologist (PhD/PsyD) or Master’s-level clinician (LMFT, LCSW, LPCC)

 - Active CA license with 7+ years post-licensure experience; open to or already holding multi-state licenses

 - Experience in pediatric behavioral health assessments assessment and evidence-based treatment

 - 3+ years managing clinical teams, including scheduling, culture, and quality

 - Skilled in improving workflows, outcomes, and patient experience

 - Collaborative, proactive, and tech-savvy

 - Culturally responsive and committed to ongoing learning

 - Equipped for secure, remote work

What You’ll Own:

Team Oversight and Leadership

 - Supervise and support a growing clinical team of psychologists and master’s level providers, ensuring the delivery of high-quality, evidence-based care.

 - Lead with empathy, transparency, and accountability—fostering a clinical culture that engages and retains high-performing individuals and maintains accountability to performance standards.

 - Champion clinical excellence and uphold the highest ethical and regulatory standards across the team.

 - Comfortable working in a lean, fast-moving team with the flexibility to support last-minute scheduling changes when needed (e.g., provider coverage or group rescheduling), as you help us build toward a more scalable, fully staffed model. This may include:
   
   - Co

source_jobs.title: Head of People Operations and Recruiting
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CLARITY PEDIATRICS

Clarity Pediatrics is a virtual pediatric specialty care company serving children with conditions like ADHD, anxiety and obesity across Medicaid and commercial populations. We serve patients in California and Texas and will enter new states in coming months. We believe great pediatric care is a force multiplier on a child's life trajectory.

Now is a great time to join - we just closed a substantial Series A, which means we have runway, ambition and a growing team. Since inception, we’ve raised $25M from leading VCs who invested early in category winners like One Medical, Hims, Spring Health, Cityblock, Alto Pharmacy, Strava, and Tia.




THE ROLE AT A GLANCE

You will build Clarity's People Operations and Recruiting function from a small, scrappy base into one that can support a Series B and the FTE growth that follows.

This is a vital role - our people build our service and our business, represent our mission and our brand. Our talent is the engine of our innovation and our business growth. Our leadership team is excited to add a peer who can partner with us to marry the people strategy with our growth strategy.

This is a player-coach position. For at least the first 12 months, you will personally run our most important searches (clinical leadership, senior functional hires) while standing up the systems, team, and playbooks to scale. This is a builder and driver role that requires high performance; we’ll expect you to regularly hit or exceed talent, people and culture targets.




WHAT YOU'LL OWN IN YEAR ONE

Your scorecard is concrete and ladders directly to our company OKRs:

Clinical talent ramp. Hire new clinicians (MD, NP, behavioral) across many states on the operating plan timeline. Clinical FTE growth is a top-line company OKR — you own the People side of it end-to-end, including partnership with Provider Ops to ensure rapid credentialing and manage multi-state licensing logistics.

Recruiting engine. Hit time-to-hire targets of 2-4 weeks for clinical roles and quickly help close corporate roles. You’ll continue to deliver >90% candidate accept rates.

Multi-state expansion readiness. Build the "People playbook" for entering a new state: employment law, credentialing plans, payroll/benefits configuration, local talent market mapping.

Culture, retention and engagement. Evolved our employee pulse/eNPS program in the first 90 days. Drive regrettable clinical attrition below 10% by year-end. Stand up a manager enablement cadence — performance management, comp planning, leveling — so our managers are getting better, not just busier.

Org design and strategy. Be a thought partner to the leadership team on our clinical/ops/tech/growth dyad model: levels, spans, succession, comp framework. The org will evolve materially between now and Series B — you're a co-architect.




WHAT YOU'LL DO DAY-TO-DAY

Talent acquisition, with a focus on clinical hiring (~50% of your time in year one). Own the full recruiting lifecycle: sourcing strategy, ATS optimization, screening rubrics, interview kits, offer construction. Personally close senior clinical and cross-functional hires. Manage outside recruiters and contractors as needed; hire your second in-house recruiter when the volume justifies it.

HRBP and leadership partnership. Coach and partner with the leadership team on org design, comp, performance, and difficult people decisions. Run quarterly performance calibration. Build the leveling and comp framework we'll use through Series B.

People operations and compliance. Own HRIS, payroll, benefits, and the policy stack. Ensure compliance with multi-state employment law, HIPAA, OIG exclusion checks, BAA management for vendors, and healthcare-specific requirements that come with clinical employment. Partner with our credentialing function on provider licensing across states.

Clinical employment strategy. Partner with the medical and behavioral clinical leads on employment model design (license types, W2 v

source_jobs.title: Growth Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CLARITY PEDIATRICS

Clarity Pediatrics is reimagining pediatric chronic care. We've launched with ADHD and anxiety (conditions that affect nearly 1 in 5 children in the US), and have recently expanded into pediatric obesity care. For parents, finding the right care can feel impossible. We're building a virtual pediatric specialty clinic that delivers trusted, science-backed care to all families. And it's working. We have strong product-market fit, a growing patient base, and a clear path to expanding into additional chronic conditions.

Our leadership team has deep healthcare expertise. Our CEO has led two startups to >$100M in ARR and profitability. Our Chief Medical Officer trained at UCSF and Stanford and previously built specialty care services at Kaiser Permanente.

Now is a great time to join. We recently closed a substantial Series A, which means we have runway, ambition, and a growing team. Since inception, we've raised $25M from leading VCs who invested early in category winners like One Medical, Hims, Spring Health, Cityblock, Alto Pharmacy, Strava, and Tia.




THE ROLE

We're hiring a Growth Engineer to own product-led growth at Clarity end-to-end: research, ideation, build, ship, and measure. You'll own the surfaces that drive top-of-funnel acquisition without sales or marketing dependency. This is a builder's role with full product authority. You set the strategy, write the spec, ship the code, and own the metric.

You'll report to our Head of Product, with a dotted line to our Head of GTM. You'll work closely with clinical, operations, sales, and engineering – but you'll largely operate independently on the surfaces you own.

Your primary focus is provider referrals. We have 1,600+ referring pediatricians in our network. Your job is to make Clarity undeniably useful to them: easy to refer, automated where possible, and trustworthy through outcome data.

Your second focus is enterprise. You'll be the hands-on builder behind the integrations and partner-facing surfaces (EMR connections, custom partner experiences, payer-facing analytics) that turn exploratory conversations into real launches.




WHERE YOU'LL FOCUS

 - Discovery and user research: building systematic ways to learn from pediatricians at scale and translating insights into a prioritized growth roadmap.

 - Self-serve product experiences that make it effortless for pediatricians and their staff to navigate Clarity, refer patients, and get answers without contacting support.

 - Workflow automation that removes administrative burden from referring providers and shortens the referral path.

 - Clinical collaboration infrastructure: the two-way data flow that closes the loop between Clarity and the providers who refer to us.

 - Patient experience improvements paired with feedback loops back to referrers, so trust compounds over time.

 - Enterprise integrations and partner-facing surfaces that turn exploratory conversations into signed partnerships.
   
   


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Hybrid builder. 6+ years shipping product end-to-end. You're a strong engineer who has owned product decisions, or a product leader who writes their own code. You're equally comfortable in a PRD and a pull request.

 - Bias to ship and learn. You design experiments to falsify your strongest assumption first, not to confirm what you already believe. You can prototype in a weekend and harden for production the next week.

 - Owns the metric, not the output. You pick a north star, defend it, and report against it honestly, including when it isn't moving.

 - Genuinely enjoys talking to users. In this role, that means pediatricians, their staff, and partner teams, not just analytics dashboards.

 - Operates well solo. You'll have full ownership of the surfaces above. You set the roadmap, write the spec, ship the code, and pull in support from clinical, operations, and engineering when needed.

 - Healthcare experience is a plus. Provider workflow tools, EMR/EHR integra

source_jobs.title: Pediatric Obesity Specialist - CA License
source_jobs.description:
Clarity Pediatrics http://www.claritypediatrics.com is reimagining pediatric chronic care, starting with ADHD. After successfully launching our virtual clinic for ADHD and anxiety, we are expanding our pediatric care platform to address other common chronic conditions and are focused right now on Obesity and Overweight management. Our new clinical model is tech-enabled, backed by the best science, and designed to deliver exceptional health outcomes that transform a child's trajectory.



We are seeking a passionate Pediatrician to join our multi-disciplinary clinical team as an Obesity Specialist. In this role, you will be at the forefront of our new program, providing virtual care centered around family-based behavioral therapy and individualized medical management for children and teens (ages 5-17).



Our co-founders and leadership team have deep commitment to clinical excellence and domain expertise in healthcare. Our CEO, Christina LaMontagne https://www.linkedin.com/in/christina-lamontagne-a289152/, has led two startups and earned her MBA at Harvard Business School. Our Chief Medical Officer, Alesandro Larrazabal, MD https://www.linkedin.com/in/alesandro-larrazabal-m-d-7b4a9623/, trained at UCSF and Stanford. Until recently, he was a Medical Director building specialty care services at Kaiser Permanente. Earlier, he worked at IDEO.



What makes this role unique:

 - Integrated Care Model: Work within a multi-disciplinary team of physicians, nurse practitioners, Master’s level therapists and psychologists to provide integrated, evidence-based care.

 - Pioneer a New Program: Contribute directly to the development and iteration of our Obesity and Overweight care model, workflows, and clinical tools.

 - Professional Growth: We encourage clinicians to develop special areas of expertise and become leaders within the team. We provide ongoing training, guidance from experts, and stipends for professional development.

Responsibilities: 

 - Conduct 60-minute virtual intake appointments to screen for obesity/overweight and determine individualized treatment plans.

 - Provide comprehensive clinical services, including medication management (e.g., GLP-1s and other anti-obesity medications) and ordering/interpreting diagnostic labs.

 - Lead our "Positive Parenting for Healthy Weight" Shared Medical Appointment groups

 - Collaborate with behavioral health colleagues and refer to other services as appropriate

 - Lead 30-minute medical follow-up appointments to monitor progress and adjust treatment plans.

 - Complete clinical documentation and administrative work accurately and in a timely manner.

 - Collaborate with nursing staff on prior authorizations and care coordination.

Qualifications:

 - Licensure: Licensed to practice medicine in California with active DEA registration

 - Board Certification: Board certified in Pediatrics. Board certification in Obesity Medicine preferred.

 - Experience: At least 5 years of patient care experience.

 - Specialized Interest: Deep interest or experience in Lifestyle Medicine, nutrition coaching, and physical activity.

 - Tech-Savvy: Confidence in using electronic medical records (EMR) and telemedicine tools to deliver virtual care.

 - Cultural Responsiveness: Experience working with families from diverse backgrounds and a commitment to providing culturally responsive treatment.

 - Startup Mindset: Excitement about joining a fast-growing startup and iterating on new care models.

Nice to have:

 - Spanish-speaking (to support our upcoming Spanish-language initiatives).

 - Licensed in additional states (or open to obtaining additional licenses with our support).



Job Type: Full-time (30+ hours)

Location: Remote

Compensation and Benefits: 

 - Pay: The target salary for this role will be $220,000-$245,000/year (full-time) 

 - Competitive salary with medical and ancillary benefits, including 401(K)

 - Remote-friendly culture

 - Flexible time off

Schedule:

 - Monday through

source_jobs.title: Contract LCSW/LMFT (Remote - Arizona)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CLARITY PEDIATRICS:

Clarity Pediatrics is reimagining pediatric chronic care, starting with ADHD. Nearly 10% of people in the US are diagnosed with ADHD, and for parents, it can feel impossible to find the right care. We are building a virtual pediatric ADHD clinic that delivers trusted care to all families. Our new clinical model is tech-enabled, backed by the best science, and has the potential to deliver exceptional health outcomes to transform a child’s trajectory. In the future, our pediatric care platform will expand rapidly to other common chronic conditions like asthma, allergies, and obesity.

Our co-founders and leadership team have deep commitment to clinical excellence and domain expertise in healthcare. Our CEO, Christina LaMontagne, has led two startups and earned her MBA at Harvard Business School. Our Chief Medical Officer, Alesandro Larrazabal, MD, trained at UCSF and Stanford. Until recently, he was a Medical Director building specialty care services at Kaiser Permanente. Earlier, he worked at IDEO.

About the Role

We are seeking an Arizona-licensed Therapist to support the launch of our behavioral health services in Arizona. This contract position will assist with payer enrollment, credentialing, and clinical readiness activities as we expand access to care in the state.

This is a flexible, 1099 hourly consultant role. Clinical service opportunities may become available in the future based on mutual interest and business needs.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Credentialing & Licensure
   
   - Participate in commercial and Medicaid payer enrollment processes
   
   - Complete and maintain CAQH and credentialing documentation
   
   - Review and sign provider enrollment applications and related attestations

 - Participate in occasional meetings related to network expansion and market launch
   
   - Participate in 1-2 user research interviews to understand the AZ landscape - provider attitudes, patient attitudes towards care
   
   - Help identify local key opinion leaders in the pediatric space and facilitate introductions where appropriate


QUALIFICATIONS

 - Active, unrestricted Arizona license as an LCSW, LMHC, LMFT, or Psychologist

 - Eligible for credentialing with commercial and government payers

 - Experience delivering behavioral health services in outpatient or telehealth settings preferred

 - Strong organizational skills and timely completion of credentialing requirements

 - Interest in supporting innovative, technology-enabled care delivery models

 - Active Arizona Medicaid enrollment preferred

 - Arizona state residency required


ENGAGEMENT DETAILS

 - Independent Contractor (1099)

 - $50/hr, paid monthly via invoice

 - Flexible schedule, estimated at about 1 hour per week

 - Limited time commitment during launch phase

 - Opportunity to expand involvement as patient services launch in Arizona

source_jobs.title: Contract Pediatrician (MD, Remote - Arizona)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

We are seeking a Arizona-licensed Pediatrician to support the launch of our behavioral health and medical services in Arizona. This contract position will play a key role in payer enrollment, credentialing, clinical readiness, and launch activities as we establish operations in the state.

This is a flexible, 1099 hourly consultant role. Clinical service opportunities may become available in the future based on mutual interest and business needs.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Credentialing & Licensure
   
   - Participate in commercial and Medicaid payer enrollment processes
   
   - Complete and maintain CAQH and credentialing documentation
   
   - Review and sign provider enrollment applications and related attestations

 - Participate in occasional meetings related to network expansion and market launch
   
   - Participate in 1-2 user research interviews to understand the AZ landscape - provider attitudes, patient attitudes towards care
   
   - Help identify local key opinion leaders in the pediatric space and facilitate introductions where appropriate


QUALIFICATIONS

 - Active, unrestricted Arizona medical license

 - Board-certified Pediatrician

 - Active DEA registration

 - Ability to credential with commercial and government payers

 - Experience with telehealth and value-based care models preferred

 - Strong attention to detail and responsiveness during enrollment and credentialing activities

 - Active Arizona Medicaid enrollment preferred

 - Arizona state residency required


ENGAGEMENT DETAILS

 - Independent Contractor (1099)

 - $100/hr, paid monthly via invoice

 - Flexible schedule, estimated at about 1 hour per week

 - Limited time commitment during launch phase

 - Opportunity to expand involvement as patient services launch in Arizona

source_jobs.title: Registered Dietician - Part Time, Remote
source_jobs.description:
Clarity Pediatrics http://www.claritypediatrics.com is reimagining pediatric chronic care, starting with ADHD. Nearly 10% of people in the US are diagnosed with ADHD, and for parents, it can feel impossible to find the right care. We are building a virtual pediatric ADHD clinic that delivers trusted care to all families. Our new clinical model is tech-enabled, backed by the best science, and has the potential to deliver exceptional health outcomes to transform a child’s trajectory. In the future, our pediatric care platform will expand rapidly to other common chronic conditions like asthma, allergies, and obesity.



We are seeking a passionate and entrepreneurial Registered Dietitian to join our multi-disciplinary team as a part-time hourly W2. In this role, you will shape the nutrition and dietary programming at Clarity, lead group and/or individual sessions, and serve as an external ambassador for our growing platform.



WHAT MAKES THIS ROLE UNIQUE:



Develop Cutting-Edge Programming

Be the architect of Clarity’s nutrition and dietary curriculum. You will design evidence-based programming and protocols tailored to our families, drawing on the latest research in pediatric nutrition, family-based behavioral therapy, and motivational interviewing.



Direct Patient Impact

Lead group sessions and provide individualized dietary guidance to children and families. Work alongside a multi-disciplinary team of physicians, nurse practitioners, and behavioral health clinicians to deliver integrated, coordinated care.



Ambassador for Clarity

Represent Clarity Pediatrics externally — at conferences, in community partnerships, on social media, and in other public forums — to grow awareness of our program and reach more families who need support.




RESPONSIBILITIES: 

Programming & Curriculum Development

 - Develop and continuously refine Clarity’s nutrition and diet programming (ages 5–17), with a special emphasis on creating a new group-based series and/or individualized content.

 - Create evidence-based educational materials, meal planning resources, and behavioral frameworks for patients and families.

 - Collaborate with the clinical team to align dietary recommendations with medical and behavioral health treatment plans.

 - Contribute to the development of clinical workflows, tools, and protocols related to nutrition care.



Group Sessions & Direct Patient Care

 - Plan and lead virtual group nutrition sessions for patients and/or caregivers.

 - Conduct individual dietary consultations as part of the integrated care model.

 - Monitor patient progress, adjust recommendations, and document clinical interactions accurately and in a timely manner.

 - Collaborate with medical and behavioral health providers to support coordinated, whole-child care.



External Ambassador

 - Represent Clarity Pediatrics at professional conferences, community events, and in media opportunities.

 - Build and maintain relationships with referring providers, schools, and community organizations.

 - Contribute to thought leadership through content creation (e.g., blog posts, webinars, social media) that elevates Clarity’s brand and mission.

 - Identify partnership opportunities to expand awareness of Clarity’s services among families and the broader healthcare community.




QUALIFICATIONS:

Hard Requirements

 - Active Registered Dietitian (RD) or Registered Dietitian Nutritionist (RDN) credential.

 - Licensed/certified to practice as a dietitian in California and/or Texas.

 - Enrollment in CA or TX Medicaid (or willingness to enroll)



Experience & Skills

 - At least 3 years of clinical or community nutrition experience, with a strong preference for pediatric or family-focused settings.

 - Experience with obesity, overweight, or metabolic health management.

 - Demonstrated ability to develop and deliver nutrition programming or educational curricula.

 - Comfort facilitating group sessions.

 - Confidence using electronic medical rec

source_jobs.title: Pediatrician - CA License - Spanish
source_jobs.description:
Clarity Pediatrics http://www.claritypediatrics.com is reimagining pediatric chronic care, starting with ADHD. Nearly 10% of people in the US are diagnosed with ADHD, and for parents, it can feel impossible to find the right care. We are building a virtual pediatric ADHD clinic that delivers trusted care to all families. Our new clinical model is tech-enabled, backed by the best science, and has the potential to deliver exceptional health outcomes to transform a child’s trajectory. In the future, our pediatric care platform will expand rapidly to other common chronic conditions like asthma, allergies, and obesity.



Our co-founders and leadership team have deep commitment to clinical excellence and domain expertise in healthcare. Our CEO, Christina LaMontagne https://www.linkedin.com/in/christina-lamontagne-a289152/, has led two startups and earned her MBA at Harvard Business School. Our Chief Medical Officer, Alesandro Larrazabal, MD https://www.linkedin.com/in/alesandro-larrazabal-m-d-7b4a9623/, trained at UCSF and Stanford. Until recently, he was a Medical Director building specialty care services at Kaiser Permanente. Earlier, he worked at IDEO.


What makes this role unique:



Opportunity to join a multi-disciplinary clinical team dedicated to providing integrated care to patients and families. We aim to give our providers  the tools they need to provide excellent care including collaboration with peers, guidance from ADHD experts and researchers, and opportunities to pursue ongoing professional development.



Contribute to the development of a better pediatric  healthcare system for patients and providers. Our team is dedicated to setting clinicians up for success, and you’ll be encouraged to help us make improvements to our care models, workflows, and clinical tools in order to make your work easier and more enjoyable.



Focus on your strengths. We appreciate how different clinical backgrounds help us learn from each other and take better care of patients. We encourage our clinicians to develop special areas of interest and expertise to become leaders within the team. We provide ongoing training opportunities to gain new skills as well as stipends for additional learning and development.



Responsibilities: 

 - Provide comprehensive clinical services for children and teens with ADHD and their families, with a focus on diagnostic and medication management.

 - Collaborate with a multidisciplinary team that includes MD’s, nurse practitioners, psychologists and other clinicians to provide excellent care.

 - Complete administrative work and clinical documentation in a timely and accurate manner.

 - Contribute to implementation, evaluation and program development iteration to ensure optimal delivery of care.

 - This is a clinical position, but there may be opportunities to expand and grow into program development, and other leadership activities, depending on employee interest and company needs.



Qualifications:

 - Licensed to practice medicine in California

 - Prescribing treatment plans including administering and managing ADHD stimulant medication

 - Board certified in Pediatrics

 - At least 5 years of patient care experience

 - Spanish-speaking

 - Experience working with families from different cultural backgrounds and willingness to receive ongoing training and support in advancing your skills in delivering culturally responsive treatment

 - Interest or experience in Lifestyle Medicine. We emphasize evidence-based interventions that support children and families such as sleep hygiene, nutritional coaching and physical activity

 - Confidence in using an electronic medical record and telemedicine tools to deliver virtual care and support to families

 - Excitement about joining a startup and interest in learning new technologies and tools



Nice to have:

 - Licensed in additional states or open to obtaining additional licenses through support from Clarity Pediatrics Medical Group



Job Type: Full-tim
